message:
refactor(extension-popularity): convert to synchronous execution with curl subprocess

Replace the async runtime (asupersync RuntimeBuilder + reactor) in the
extension popularity snapshot and scoring binaries with synchronous
curl-based HTTP calls via std::process::Command. This eliminates the
heavyweight async runtime dependency for what are fundamentally sequential
batch operations.

Key changes:
- ext_popularity_snapshot.rs: remove asupersync runtime setup; convert
  run() from async to sync; add resolve_github_token() helper that falls
  back to `gh auth token` when the env var is unset; replace
  fetch_npm_downloads/fetch_npm_registry_meta/fetch_github_repo_metrics
  with parse_*_response() functions that operate on pre-fetched data
- ext_score_candidates.rs: parallel changes for scoring pipeline
- extension_popularity.rs: add POPULARITY_REQUEST_TIMEOUT (20s) constant
  for consistent timeout enforcement across HTTP operations

This makes the binaries faster to start, easier to debug, and removes the
asupersync compile-time dependency from the extension tooling path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
